reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification): Phase 03's code reading is factually correct. The handler at rpc_methods.rs:408-460 processes DataColumnsByRoot requests without validating the total column count against MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS (16384). In contrast, DataColumnsByRange has this validation at line 1148. Gate 4 (Exploitability): An attacker can trigger this via P2P by sending a DataColumnsByRoot request with 128 identifiers (the decode-time limit at codec.rs:567), each containing 128 columns, totaling 16384 columns. While this equals (not exceeds) the limit, the complete absence of validation means no rejection occurs, and the node processes all requested columns. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): The 01e property from file outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (lines 588-604) explicitly requires: 'data_column_sidecars_by_root must reject requests exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS' with assertion 'forall req: sum(len(r.columns) for r in req) > MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS => rejected(req)'. The code violates this requirement by not checking the sum. Severity: Medium per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json criteria—this enables resource exhaustion DoS affecting >5% of the network (single-client bug in Lighthouse with ~33% network share caps severity at Medium per deployment context rules). Attack path: attacker sends crafted P2P packet → node processes 16384 columns without validation → excessive memory/CPU consumption for data retrieval and potential reconstruction.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: data_column_sidecars_by_root must reject requests exceeding MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS. [Assertion: forall req: sum(len(r.columns) for r in req) > MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS => rejected(req)]
